title: Trispzest

{{Quote|You could be the finest warrior in creation—on the ground—but now you are in my element!|Kharys|<ref name="Star Wars Annual (1977) 1">[[Star Wars Annual (1977) 1|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 1]]</ref>}}
A combination of elements of [[Form VII/Legends|Form VII]] and traditional [[S'kytri]] aerial dueling, '''Trispzest''' was a type of aerial [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. The name meant ''heart palpitation'' in the [[S'kytric]] language.<ref name="saga4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}}</ref>

==History==
It was originally developed by the airborne [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Magister of Skye|Majestrix]] of [[Skye/Legends|Skye]], [[Kharys]].<ref name="saga4" />

Trispzest would allow the combatant to utilize the advantages of flying in battle against grounded units - whom the S'kytri would derogatively refer to as "walkers". In Trispzest emphasis was placed on the use of the ''[[Sai cha/Legends|sai cha]]'' technique to decapitate "walkers".<ref name="saga4" />

Among the few known users of trispzest were [[Nazish]] and the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]], who had [[Sai tok/Legends|lost his legs]] and [[Telekinesis/Legends|levitated]] thanks to the [[dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="saga4" />

==Techniques==
[[File:Maw_kyle.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Airborne [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]] using Trispzest in a duel with [[Kyle Katarn]].]]

''Sai cha'', or decapitation, was often used against grounded combatants<ref name="saga4" /> creating strategic parallels between Trispzest and [[mounted lightsaber combat]].

Another technique, which was especially used by Maw, was a whirlwind attack, consisted of spinning wildly in the air, often while hovering in one spot, while lashing out to all sides with the lightsaber;<ref name="saga4" /> a technique that undoubtedly was efficient against multiple opponents.

Trispzest had many advantages, but one fatal weakness, as the combatant was open to attacks from directly below him or her by grounded units. Kyle Katarn took advantage of this weakness when, dropping to the ground and lunging his lightsaber upwards as Maw passed over him, he injured Maw during a duel on the [[Valley Tower]] of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]].<ref name="Dark Forces: Jedi Knight">''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''</ref>
